_definition.ISO_639_3_eng: A set of role-indicator enumeration elements that classify the functional roles a term plays in the dictionary. Each element identifies one role — such as descriptor, namespace, enumeration root, or object definition. A single term may hold multiple roles simultaneously.

_description.ISO_639_3_eng:
[`_kind_term`](_kind_term.md) is a property of the [_domn](_domn.md) section. Its value is a set of enumeration elements drawn from the [`_kind_term`](_kind_term.md) controlled vocabulary, each identifying one functional role the term plays in the dictionary.

A term may hold several roles at the same time. For example, a term can simultaneously be a **namespace** (other terms use its [`_gid`](_gid.md) as their [`_nid`](_nid.md)), an **enumeration root** (it is the root of a controlled vocabulary graph), and a **descriptor** (it carries a [`_data`](_data.md) section). Recording all roles explicitly in [`_kind_term`](_kind_term.md) allows any of them to be queried directly — without traversing the graph — which is useful for tooling and validation pipelines.

Typical role values include:

- **[`_kind_term_descriptor`](_kind_term_descriptor.md)** — the term carries a [`_data`](_data.md) section and defines the type of a data variable.
- **[`_kind_term_namespace`](_kind_term_namespace.md)** — the term acts as a namespace; other terms reference its [`_gid`](_gid.md) in their [`_nid`](_nid.md).
- **[`_kind_term_enum`](_kind_term_enum.md)** — the term is the root of a controlled vocabulary (enumeration) graph.
- **[`_kind_term_enum_element`](_kind_term_enum_element.md)** — the term is a valid selectable element within a controlled vocabulary.
- **[`_kind_term_object`](_kind_term_object.md)** — the term carries a [`_rule`](_rule.md) section and defines an object schema.
- **[`_kind_term_alias`](_kind_term_alias.md)** — the term is an alias for another term; it carries no [`_info`](_info.md) and delegates to the canonical term.
- **[`_kind_term_predicate`](_kind_term_predicate.md)** — the term is used as a predicate in an edge, qualifying the nature of a relationship between two nodes.

The set of recognised role values is defined as the [`_kind_term`](_kind_term.md) enumeration graph and may be extended as the dictionary evolves.

_definition.ISO_639_3_eng: An enumeration element indicating that the term is a descriptor — a term that carries a [`_data`](_data.md) section defining the type, shape, and constraints of a data variable. Descriptors are the fundamental building blocks used to type the properties of objects and the columns of datasets.

An enumeration element indicating that the term acts as a namespace — other terms reference its [`_gid`](_gid.md) in their [`_nid`](_nid.md) field to declare themselves as members of that namespace. A namespace term organises related terms under a common identifier prefix.

An enumeration element indicating that the term is the root of a controlled vocabulary graph — an enumeration type from which valid selectable values are drawn. Other terms connect to it via [`_predicate_enum-of`](_predicate_enum-of.md) edges to declare themselves as valid choices.

An enumeration element indicating that the term is a valid selectable value within a controlled vocabulary — connected to an enumeration root via a [`_predicate_enum-of`](_predicate_enum-of.md) edge, directly or transitively through section nodes.

An enumeration element indicating that the term is an object definition — a term that carries a [`_rule`](_rule.md) section defining the schema of a structured object: which properties are required, recommended, forbidden, or automatically managed.

An enumeration element indicating that the term is a type definition — a reusable data type descriptor that other descriptors may reference by key instead of repeating its data definition inline. A typedef term carries a concrete [`_data`](_data.md) section with an inline shape definition and may also carry a [`_rule`](_rule.md) section.

An enumeration element indicating that the term is an alias — an alternative identifier for a canonical term. An alias carries only a [`_code`](_code.md) section and no [`_info`](_info.md); the dictionary resolves it to the canonical term it points to.

An enumeration element indicating that the term is used as a predicate in an edge document — its [`_gid`](_gid.md) appears as the value of the [`_predicate`](_predicate.md) property, qualifying the nature of the relationship between the [`_from`](_from.md) and [`_to`](_to.md) nodes.
